name: American Samoa (United States)

colors.symbolism: loyalty (a traditional Samoan colour shared with the United States)
the gold of the eagle's talons grasping the Samoan symbols of authority

description: A dark blue field divided by a white triangle edged in red, the triangle's base along the fly and its apex pointing toward the hoist. Centred over the white triangle, on the fly side, is a brown American bald eagle clutching a yellow uatogi (war club) and fue (fly-whisk) in its talons.
history: Adopted on 17 April 1960, replacing the United States flag for local use. The design was selected following a competition; the configuration emphasizes the territorial relationship by combining traditional Samoan and American symbols. The flag-day anniversary on 17 April is a major civic holiday.

features.symbolism: a white triangle with red fimbriation, with its base on the fly edge and its apex pointing toward the hoist; the dark blue field above and below the triangle. The colours red, white, and blue are the traditional colours of Samoa and the United States.

the bald eagle (United States national symbol) flies toward the hoist, clutching in its talons two traditional Samoan emblems of chiefly authority: a uatogi (war club) symbolizing the power of the government, and a fue (fly-whisk) symbolizing the wisdom of traditional Samoan leadership.

features.role: uatogi (war club) held by the eagle

features.symbolism: the wisdom of traditional Samoan chieftainship.
